Title: Fez Winate Plate | Handcrafted Fez Clay Pottery – Natural Pigments & Traditional Moroccan Craftsmanship
Description:
Step into the exclusive world of Fez Black Painted Pottery, where history doesn't just repeat itself; it gets a stylish upgrade every time. Born in the 15th century and still kicking, this collection is the last of its kind, stubbornly sticking to its labor-intensive roots because, let's face it, true artistry never takes shortcuts. It's where Moroccan zest meets Andalusian flair, with a dash of Arab sophistication, a sprinkle of African spirit, European elegance, and Berber boldness—all mixed into one fabulous cultural cocktail.

Time to make this beautiful plate: (fewer hours less for small sizes)

  • Concept Design: 4 Hours
  • Clay Formation: 48 hours when sunny. 72-96 hours when rainy
  • On Clay Drawing: 1 - 3 hours depend on size
  • Coloring and firing: 36 hours
  • Drying: 24 hours when sunny and 48-72 hours days when rainy
  • Total Hours to produce this beautiful piece: 116 hours

This isn't just pottery; it's a bohemian rhapsody, wrapped in a stylish enigma, dressed in traditional threads but with a modern twist. Crafted by artisans who've inherited more than just their father's eyes—they've got generations of trade secrets running through their veins. Thanks to the local mineral spa and that snooty grey clay exclusive to the region, we've unlocked the secret recipes to hues of blue and royal green that were once only the eye candy of Moroccan royal palaces. Now, we're democratizing luxury, because why should royals have all the fun? Get ready to add a touch of exclusive, handcrafted history to your home with our Fez Black Painted Pottery—it's tradition with a trendy twist.

Title: Handcrafted Flambeau Red Moroccan Candle
Description:
Modular | Refillable | Sculptural | Indoor & Outdoor

This isn’t just a candle.
It’s a sculpture of light and earth — bold, boho, and beautifully imperfect.

At the center is a hand-blown glass bowl, made to cradle your choice of pre-cut wax or sand powder candle. Both are refillable, so you can switch scents, change moods, and create new rituals — again and again.

That bowl rests on a base of your choice:
– Cool, industrial metal
– Warm, textured travertine
– Or sleek, modern perplex glass

And rising from below: a three-leg wood stand, handcrafted in Morocco from native trees like olive, cedar, and orange wood. Each leg is cut by hand, and as we shape them, we preserve their organic curves and textures — no two legs are ever identical.

They’re perfectly imperfect, each one full of character, yet designed to come together in harmony — offering both balance and beauty. A grounded, boho-elegant form that feels as much like furniture as it does like flame.

Choose from:
– Natural wood grain
– Matte blue or white, painted with water-based vegetable paint
– Or your own custom color, sealed with a non-toxic, water-based finish

Currently available in 1.6m to 1.8m height — or made to your preferred dimensions.
Designed for indoor and outdoor use, the wood is breathable, the flame visible, the scent unforgettable.

Each piece is a one-of-a-kind collaboration between nature and artisan.
A candle that doesn’t just light the room — it changes the room.

Because beauty isn’t symmetry.
It’s soul.

Title: Handcrafted Nora Beige Moroccan Candle
Description:
This isn’t just a candle.
It’s a piece of Morocco. A piece of you. A ritual, wrapped in clay.

Each one is hand-shaped in Marrakesh using organic red clay — no molds, no machines, just generations of skill passed through hands. The pot comes with a removable lid, turning from candle to planter, flame to earth, as your space and seasons change.

We chose the finishes with care:

  • Olive green, for nature and peace — a nod to Morocco’s olive trees and the heart of Mediterranean life

  • Ivory cream and matte black, to ground you — soft neutrals for calm, timeless spaces

  • And raw terracotta, untouched and honest, glazed only on the inside to hold water and memory

Each candle is refillable, because beauty should be lasting.
The wax is a lush, creamy blend of coconut and sunflower oils — no synthetics, no toxins, just plants.

The scents are real, too.
Distilled from herbs, flowers, and spices in collaboration with a farm outside Marrakesh.
From orange blossom to Royal Oud, every note tells a story — of souks, sea air, desert nights.

We offer three forms:
– Pre-poured wax
– Clean-burning gel for quiet nights
– And playful, colored sand powder to mark seasons and moments — gold, red, green, citronella for summer evenings in Florida

This is a limited collection of 50. Once it’s gone, it’s yours alone.
No two are the same.
Just like no two homes — no two people — are the same.

Because home isn’t a place.
It’s a feeling.
And you deserve to feel something real.

Title: Verger | Large Moroccan terracotta statement designer vase
Description:
Nature, Captured.
This vase doesn’t just sit in a room — it anchors it.

Crafted by hand from Marrakech’s rich red clay, these decorative vases are sun-dried, sand-textured, and naturally pigmented in tones that bring harmony and depth to any space. No gloss. No gimmicks. Just natural color and quiet beauty.

Each piece is fired at 600°C (1112°F) — just enough to preserve the integrity of the clay while keeping its breathability. Yes, these vases literally breathe with you, responding to humidity and temperature like any natural material should.

Offered in three sizes, from 30 cm (11.8") to 200 cm (78.7"), and finished in an array of soulful tones:  Cream Black Olive Green Beige White

Their sculptural, imperfect silhouettes make them the kind of pieces people stop to touch — and ask about.

Title: Tad Serena | Handmade Moroccan Tadelakt sculptural vase set
Description:
Time in Its Purest Form
Before it becomes a vase, it’s just earth. Clay, water, limestone, olive oil — nothing more.

Each piece is built by hand, slowly and intentionally, using ancestral Moroccan methods that require the artisan to wait, not rush. Formed in stages, each layer must dry completely under the sun before the next is added. Depending on the season, this takes days — sometimes weeks. Nature sets the timeline. The artisan simply listens.

Once the form is complete, it's sealed using Tadelakt, Morocco’s iconic plastering technique once reserved for royal palaces. Polished smooth and finished with natural pigments, the surface feels as luxurious as stone, yet organic in every way.

There’s no carving. No noise. Just quiet mastery. A minimalist form with maximum presence. It’s not about decoration — it’s about depth.

Title: Chisel Najma | Handmade Moroccan artisan sculptural clay vase
Description:
Earth, Fire & Edge
There’s no automation here. No molds. No copy-paste.
This vase is made the way things used to be made — with time, tension, and truth.

Formed by hand from natural Moroccan clay, each piece is built layer by layer, with sunlight as the only clock. Rushed clay cracks. Rushed work fails. So the artisans wait. They listen. They let the earth speak.

When it’s ready, it’s fired in a centuries-old kiln, where flame meets form at 1000°C (1832°F).

Then, with a tool no bigger than a fingertip, the carving begins. Abstract. Intentional. Irreversible. One misstep and it’s over.
This is art you don’t just see — you feel its risk, its rhythm, its legacy.

Every chisel mark carries the weight of tradition and the energy of reinvention. It’s not décor.
It’s the soul of craftsmanship — made to last, made to speak.

Their sculptural, imperfect silhouettes make them the kind of pieces people stop to touch — and ask about.

Title: Moro | Natural Terracotta Plant Pot – Handmade Clay Planter for Home & Garden
Description:
MOROCCAN EARTH PLANTER

Raw | Breathable | Layer-Built | Indoor & Outdoor

This is what clay looks like when you leave it alone —
no paint, no glaze, no cover-up.
Just raw, natural beauty, pulled from the earth and brought to life by hand.

Each planter is made from our exclusive blend of four Moroccan clays:
— Marrakech red for depth
— Northern white for softness
— Saharan grey for strength
— Fez yellow for heritage

The color you see? It’s earned — not mixed in a lab.
Each batch is shaped by season, soil, and the rhythm of the land.

Every planter is layer-built by hand, 20 cm at a time.
Then sun-dried slowly, and fired at 1000°C to lock in strength and soul.

Five designs. Three sizes. Infinite character.
For homes that want to feel closer to nature —
from Marrakesh to Palm Beach.

Title: Douce Candle in Recycled Glass – Handmade in Marrakesh with Botanical Oils
Description:
Not every candle has to look like a candle.
And not every moment needs to feel the same.

This is our small glassware collection — mouth-blown by artisans in Morocco, each piece clear, clean, and quietly beautiful. Designed to be lightweight, versatile, and expressive, these pieces let you set the tone — not follow one.

Fill it with sand powder candles in bold, seasonal colors.
Or place a flameless gel candle inside for a calm, scent-forward moment.
No fixed wax. No long wait. No single scent taking over your space.
Just freedom to switch things up — from room to room, mood to mood.

All our scents are made from pure essential oils, distilled in small batches with Moroccan farms and labs who share our respect for the earth.
Scents like Royal Oud, Ocean Soft, Orange Blossom, Verveine, and Oriental Desert — each one a story, each one a feeling.

And when you’re done?
Just close the lid. The scent stays sealed, fresh, and ready for next time.
No oxidizing. No waste.

This is candlelight that adapts.
Glassware that invites you to play.
Scent that stays fresh, flexible, and fully yours.

Because beauty shouldn't box you in.
It should open you up.

Title: T / 09 | Moroccan Wool Rug
Description:
Discover the beauty of authentic handmade Moroccan rugs, each one handwoven from pure, natural wool and colored with earth-derived pigments for a soft, organic tone. Our designs capture the essence of minimalism — often in single-color, design-free styles — yet we also offer modern geometric patterns, plush shag textures, and timeless area rugs to suit every space.

Whether you’re searching for a cozy Moroccan runner for your hallway, a thick-pile wool rug for your living room, or a versatile Moroccan-style rug in custom dimensions, each piece is sustainably made and entirely unique. The craft of weaving is carried out slowly and intentionally, creating rugs that bring warmth, texture, and grounding presence to your home.

From washable and outdoor-friendly styles to luxurious shag and trellis designs, our collection celebrates Morocco’s weaving heritage while offering options for both contemporary and traditional interiors. These are more than floor coverings — they are enduring works of art that will be part of your home’s story for years to come.

Lead Time

Each and every Dary rug is woven entirely by hand and then washed and dried in the mountain air. Current lead times are:

Up to 9' x 12' | 18 - 20 weeks

9' x 12' - 12' x 15' | 20 - 22 weeks

12' x 15' and larger | Up to 26 weeks

Title: Handcrafted Nora Vert Moroccan Candle
Description:
This isn’t just a candle.
It’s a piece of Morocco. A piece of you. A ritual, wrapped in clay.

Each one is hand-shaped in Marrakesh using organic red clay — no molds, no machines, just generations of skill passed through hands. The pot comes with a removable lid, turning from candle to planter, flame to earth, as your space and seasons change.

We chose the finishes with care:

  • Olive green, for nature and peace — a nod to Morocco’s olive trees and the heart of Mediterranean life

  • Ivory cream and matte black, to ground you — soft neutrals for calm, timeless spaces

  • And raw terracotta, untouched and honest, glazed only on the inside to hold water and memory

Each candle is refillable, because beauty should be lasting.
The wax is a lush, creamy blend of coconut and sunflower oils — no synthetics, no toxins, just plants.

The scents are real, too.
Distilled from herbs, flowers, and spices in collaboration with a farm outside Marrakesh.
From orange blossom to Royal Oud, every note tells a story — of souks, sea air, desert nights.

We offer three forms:
– Pre-poured wax
– Clean-burning gel for quiet nights
– And playful, colored sand powder to mark seasons and moments — gold, red, green, citronella for summer evenings in Florida

This is a limited collection of 50. Once it’s gone, it’s yours alone.
No two are the same.
Just like no two homes — no two people — are the same.

Because home isn’t a place.
It’s a feeling.
And you deserve to feel something real.

Title: Handcrafted Flambeau Noyer Moroccan Candle
Description:
Modular | Refillable | Sculptural | Indoor & Outdoor

This isn’t just a candle.
It’s a sculpture of light and earth — bold, boho, and beautifully imperfect.

At the center is a hand-blown glass bowl, made to cradle your choice of pre-cut wax or sand powder candle. Both are refillable, so you can switch scents, change moods, and create new rituals — again and again.

That bowl rests on a base of your choice:
– Cool, industrial metal
– Warm, textured travertine
– Or sleek, modern perplex glass

And rising from below: a three-leg wood stand, handcrafted in Morocco from native trees like olive, cedar, and orange wood. Each leg is cut by hand, and as we shape them, we preserve their organic curves and textures — no two legs are ever identical.

They’re perfectly imperfect, each one full of character, yet designed to come together in harmony — offering both balance and beauty. A grounded, boho-elegant form that feels as much like furniture as it does like flame.

Choose from:
– Natural wood grain
– Matte blue or white, painted with water-based vegetable paint
– Or your own custom color, sealed with a non-toxic, water-based finish

Currently available in 1.6m to 1.8m height — or made to your preferred dimensions.
Designed for indoor and outdoor use, the wood is breathable, the flame visible, the scent unforgettable.

Each piece is a one-of-a-kind collaboration between nature and artisan.
A candle that doesn’t just light the room — it changes the room.

Because beauty isn’t symmetry.
It’s soul.

Title: Flottant D'eau | Floating Moroccan Tagine Pool Candle – Refillable Terracotta & Wood with Invisible Stabilizing Weight
Description:
Inspired by Heritage | Made for Celebration | Designed to Float

In Morocco, the tagine isn’t just a cooking vessel — it’s a symbol of gathering, warmth, and home.
So we took that symbol and gave it new life. On water.

The Floating Pool Candle Tagine brings the soul of Moroccan design to your most memorable moments — birthdays, weddings, summer nights under the stars.

Crafted entirely from 100% Moroccan wood, each piece cradles a natural clay candle holder at its center — a small cylindrical form made by hand from organic red clay. It holds a clean-burning, refillable candle, available in our signature sand powder or pre-poured wax forms.

It doesn’t sit on the pool’s edge.
It floats on the water — weightless, elegant, anchored.

Attach it to our custom resin weight base (invisible below the surface) to keep it in place, or let it drift softly as part of your decor. No more knocking over candles. No more cluttering your poolside. Just clean, ambient light — moving with the water.

Every piece is a tribute to tradition — reimagined for modern celebration.

Because light shouldn’t only surround the pool.
It should live on it.

Title: Jomana Candle in Recycled Glass – Handmade in Marrakesh with Botanical Oils
Description:
Not every candle has to look like a candle.
And not every moment needs to feel the same.

This is our small glassware collection — mouth-blown by artisans in Morocco, each piece clear, clean, and quietly beautiful. Designed to be lightweight, versatile, and expressive, these pieces let you set the tone — not follow one.

Fill it with sand powder candles in bold, seasonal colors.
Or place a flameless gel candle inside for a calm, scent-forward moment.
No fixed wax. No long wait. No single scent taking over your space.
Just freedom to switch things up — from room to room, mood to mood.

All our scents are made from pure essential oils, distilled in small batches with Moroccan farms and labs who share our respect for the earth.
Scents like Royal Oud, Ocean Soft, Orange Blossom, Verveine, and Oriental Desert — each one a story, each one a feeling.

This is candlelight that adapts.
Glassware that invites you to play.
Scent that stays fresh, flexible, and fully yours.

Because beauty shouldn't box you in.
It should open you up.

Description:
Mouth-Blown Glass Candle — A Ritual, Reinvented

You’re not one thing.
Your home shouldn’t be either.

This isn’t just a candle — it’s an invitation to create.
Each piece is mouth-blown by Moroccan glassmakers, clear and transparent by default, but never empty.

You can fill it with anything:
Flowers. Water. Sand. Stones. Memory. Mood.
Then top it with one of our signature candle forms — wax, gel, or sand — and make the moment yours.

We’ve also created a painted glass collection, using non-toxic water-based vegetable paints in red, blue, black, and white — giving you a bold, boho-cool aesthetic for spaces that want a little more personality.

Because we believe you should never be stuck with one look, one scent, one feeling.

Our refillable system lets you switch between:
– Pre-cut wax candles, ready to light
– Biodegradable flameless gel, for clean scent in shared or quiet spaces
– And vibrant sand powders, in colors like gold, green, red, and blue — perfect for seasonal decorating or celebrating

All our scents are distilled from real essential oils.
No synthetic fragrance. No fillers. Just nature, captured.
Scents like Orange Blossom, Ocean Soft, Royal Oud, Verveine, and Oriental Desert — made to match your space, your energy, and your needs.

This is not a candle that burns and disappears.
It’s one that evolves — with your mood, your season, your story.

Because light isn’t static.
And your home shouldn’t be either.

Title: Hayati Mouth-Blown Moroccan Tea Glass Candle – Refillable & Scented
Description:
The Hayati Candle is a modern tribute to the classic Moroccan tea glass — a small, transparent cup found in every Moroccan home and café. Handcrafted using mouth-blown recycled glass, this tea glass has been thoughtfully transformed into a refillable candle holder that blends cultural tradition with modern sustainability.

Each holder is partially filled with clear resin, preserving the glass’s shape and adding a striking visual depth. The top is left open for a perfectly pre-shaped candle insert, made from natural coconut and sunflower wax. Choose from unscented or explore our full range of organic scented refills, crafted from pure essential oils and distilled botanicalssourced from our Moroccan farms in Marrakesh and Ourika.

Want flexibility? Opt for a multi-scent candle set — each refill burns for just over an hour, allowing you to match the scent to your mood, your ritual, or your setting. It’s perfect for a 60-minute spa session, meditation, or mindful winding down after a long day.

The Hayati Candle offers more than light — it offers intentional living, sensory elevation, and a deep connection to Moroccan artistry.

Features:

  • Handmade & mouth-blown in Morocco

  • Inspired by the iconic Moroccan tea glass

  • Filled with resin for a unique aesthetic and structure

  • Includes refillable wax inserts (scented or unscented)

  • Made from natural wax and organic essential oils

  • Optional multi-scented refills for mood-based rotation

  • Each candle burns ~1 hour — ideal for spa or meditation sessions

Perfect for:
Luxury home décor, Moroccan-style interiors, handmade gifts, spas, wellness retreats, and anyone seeking a refillable, clean-burning, and beautifully designed candle experience.

Title: Epure Tagine | Clay Candle – Handmade in Marrakesh with Botanical Oils & Refillable Liquid Wax
Description:
Cultural Icon | Multifunctional | Customizable | Made in Morocco

The tagine is more than a shape.
It’s a Moroccan symbol of gathering, sharing, and soulfulness — and now, it holds a new kind of warmth.

Our Clay Tagine Candle is handmade from organic Moroccan clay, and shaped into a miniature version of the iconic vessel. It’s a conversation piece, a statement object, and a functional candle, all in one.

Available in three signature tones —
– Bold red,
– Deep blue,
– And soft creamy beige —
Each one evokes a different side of Morocco’s palette, from desert spice to ocean to medina walls.

It comes in all three of our wax forms:
– Pre-poured wax, ready to burn
– Sand powder, playful and expressive
– And biodegradable flameless gel for quiet moments of scent

When the candle is done, the story doesn’t end.
Use the tagine to hold jewelry, chocolates, dried herbs, or whatever moment calls for beauty and intention. It’s multifunctional, refillable, and designed to live on your table, console, or shelf as a symbol of hospitality and elegance.

We offer custom color options for those seeking a personalized piece — perfect for gifts, events, or matching your space.

Because sometimes the smallest things —
carry the deepest roots.

Title: Biodegradable Gel Wax
Description:
A Flame-Free Scent Experience. Beautiful. Safe. Clean.

Our biodegradable gel candle wax is a clean, modern alternative to traditional candles — offering a continuous scented experience without a flame. Ideal for bathrooms, bedrooms, nurseries, or any space where safety and serenity are a priority, especially in homes with young children or pets.

Naturally crystal-clear, the gel takes on the hue of the essential oil scent added to it — creating a soft, personalized glow. Whether placed inside a transparent glass holder or your favorite vessel, it becomes both scent and décor.

  • Flame-Free + Worry-Free: No wick, no open flame — just scent that lingers.

  • Subtle Glow, Natural Look: Clear gel absorbs the tint of your scent oil for a soft, elegant hue.

  • Eco-Conscious: Biodegradable formula, safe for you and the planet.

  • Slow Release: Each box includes 2 gel servings; each serving lasts 3–4 weeks.

  • Weight: 90g (0.4 lbs) total — each serving approx. 45g.

Title: A / 03 | Handcrafted Moroccan Wool Rug
Description:
Discover the beauty of authentic handmade Moroccan rugs, each one handwoven from pure, natural wool and colored with earth-derived pigments for a soft, organic tone. Our designs capture the essence of minimalism — often in single-color, design-free styles — yet we also offer modern geometric patterns, plush shag textures, and timeless area rugs to suit every space.

Title: Tad Naki Vase | Handmade Moroccan Tadelakt lime plaster vase
Description:
Time in Its Purest Form. Before it becomes a vase, it’s just earth. Clay, water, limestone, olive oil — nothing more.

Each piece is built by hand, slowly and intentionally, using ancestral Moroccan methods that require the artisan to wait, not rush. Formed in stages, each layer must dry completely under the sun before the next is added. Depending on the season, this takes days — sometimes weeks. Nature sets the timeline. The artisan simply listens.

Once the form is complete, it's sealed using Tadelakt, Morocco’s iconic plastering technique once reserved for royal palaces. Polished smooth and finished with natural pigments, the surface feels as luxurious as stone, yet organic in every way.

There’s no carving. No noise. Just quiet mastery. A minimalist form with maximum presence. It’s not about decoration — it’s about depth.

Title: Tam Ozoli Gola | Moroccan Tamegroute green pottery jug
Description:
Green Terracotta Clay Tamagroute Gola Jug.
 
Behold the glaze that dazzles, the color that captivates, the shape that seduces, and the simplicity that speaks volumes. But wait, there's more! It's the story woven into each piece that truly sets it apart, offering you an exclusive hotline to a land shrouded in mystery. This isn't just pottery; it's your passport to an adventure in a realm seldom tread, where every curve tells a tale and every hue hints at a hidden world. Prepare to be connected, not just to a piece of art, but to the very essence of a land whispered about in the annals of the unknown.
 
Size and color may vary slightly as each piece is hand-thrown and hand-glazed. 
 
Versatile and Timeless Decor:
Whether gracing a home kitchen, a grand mansion, a company lobby, or a hotel, these products are versatile. They are a perfect choice for interior designers, decor enthusiasts, travelers with fond Moroccan memories, or anyone who appreciates the artistry of handmade, eco-friendly products.
      

Time to make this beautiful jug:

  • Concept Design: 0.5 Hours
  • Clay Formation: 48 hours when sunny. 72-96 hours when rainy
  • Coloring and firing: 36 hours
  • Drying: 24 hours when sunny and 48-72 hours days when rainy
  • Total Hours to produce this beautiful piece: 151 hours

Title: Stella Emerald Hana Plate | Handcrafted Marrakesh Clay Pottery – Natural Pigments & Traditional Moroccan Craftsmanship
Description:
Discover our exquisite collection of handmade plates, brought to life by the skilled hands of Moroccan artisans. Crafted from the finest white clay, each plate celebrates the essence of Moroccan elegance with its unique shape. The design features delicately engraved mint branches, a homage to mint's integral role in Moroccan culture. Infused with hues of Sky Blue and Emerald, the plates evoke a sense of serene tranquility, reminiscent of Morocco's clear skies and lush landscapes. This artistic vision, conceived by French designer Stella during her visit to Morocco, marries traditional artisanship with contemporary flair. Ideal for serving or as decorative accents, these plates are a testament to unique craftsmanship and modern design.

Please note, due to their handmade nature, size and color may vary, enhancing the individuality of each piece.

For Aficionados of Authenticity and Elegance:
This collection is designed for those who cherish the beauty of uniqueness and simplicity in their décor. Each item stands out with its single color palette, embracing imperfections in shape and hue that add character and depth. These pieces, with their bohemian style, showcase a refined elegance, perfect for those who value the authentic touch of artisan craftsmanship combined with a modern minimalist aesthetic.

Versatility in Design:
Our White Clay collection is designed to fit seamlessly into any space – be it a cozy home, an elegant mansion, a modern office lobby, or a luxurious hotel. It's an ideal choice for interior designers, decor aficionados, or those who hold a special place in their hearts for Moroccan culture.
 

Time to make this beautiful plate: 

  • Concept Design: 3 hours
  • Clay Formation: 48 hours when sunny. 72-96 hours when rainy
  • Mint Branch Itching on Clay: 0.5 hours
  • Coloring and firing: 36 hours
  • Drying: 24 hours when sunny and 48-72 hours days when rainy
  • Total Hours to produce this beautiful piece: 111.5 hours

Title: Fez Chisel Turquoise Plate | Handcrafted Fez Clay Pottery – Natural Pigments & Traditional Moroccan Craftsmanship
Description:
Step into the exclusive world of Fez Chisel Pottery, where history doesn't just repeat itself; it gets a stylish upgrade every time. Born in the 15th century and still kicking, this collection is the last of its kind, stubbornly sticking to its labor-intensive roots because, let's face it, true artistry never takes shortcuts. It's where Moroccan zest meets Andalusian flair, with a dash of Arab sophistication, a sprinkle of African spirit, European elegance, and Berber boldness—all mixed into one fabulous cultural cocktail.

Time to make this beautiful plate (fewer hours for smaller sizes):

  • Concept Design: 2 Hours

  • Coloring and firing: 36 hours
  • Drying: 24 hours when sunny and 48-72 hours days when rainy

Thanks to the local mineral spa and that snooty grey clay exclusive to the region, we've unlocked the secret recipes to hues of blue and royal green that were once only the eye candy of Moroccan royal palaces. Now, we're democratizing luxury, because why should royals have all the fun? Get ready to add a touch of exclusive, handcrafted history to your home with our Fez Chisel Pottery—it's tradition with a trendy twist.

Vintage Green Glazed Terracotta Clay Tamagroute Glila.
 
Behold the glaze that dazzles, the color that captivates, the shape that seduces, and the simplicity that speaks volumes. But wait, there's more! Versatile and Timeless Decor:
Whether gracing a home kitchen, a grand mansion, a company lobby, or a hotel, these products are versatile. They are a perfect choice for interior designers, decor enthusiasts, travelers with fond Moroccan memories, or anyone who appreciates the artistry of handmade, eco-friendly products.

Time to make this beautiful vase:

  • Clay Formation: 48 hours when sunny. 72-96 hours when rainy

Title: Tad Lona Vase | Moroccan Tadelakt rounded designer plaster vase
Description:
Time in Its Purest Form
Before it becomes a vase, it’s just earth. Clay, water, limestone, olive oil — nothing more.

Each piece is built by hand, slowly and intentionally, using ancestral Moroccan methods that require the artisan to wait, not rush. Formed in stages, each layer must dry completely under the sun before the next is added. Depending on the season, this takes days — sometimes weeks. Nature sets the timeline. The artisan simply listens.

Once the form is complete, it's sealed using Tadelakt, Morocco’s iconic plastering technique once reserved for royal palaces. Polished smooth and finished with natural pigments, the surface feels as luxurious as stone, yet organic in every way.

There’s no carving. No noise. Just quiet mastery. A minimalist form with maximum presence. It’s not about decoration — it’s about depth.

Title: Tad Krisha Vase | Moroccan Tadelakt statement sculptural plaster vase
Description:
Time in Its Purest Form
Before it becomes a vase, it’s just earth. Clay, water, limestone, olive oil — nothing more.

Each piece is built by hand, slowly and intentionally, using ancestral Moroccan methods that require the artisan to wait, not rush. Formed in stages, each layer must dry completely under the sun before the next is added. Depending on the season, this takes days — sometimes weeks. Nature sets the timeline. The artisan simply listens.

Once the form is complete, it's sealed using Tadelakt, Morocco’s iconic plastering technique once reserved for royal palaces. Polished smooth and finished with natural pigments, the surface feels as luxurious as stone, yet organic in every way.

There’s no carving. No noise. Just quiet mastery. A minimalist form with maximum presence. It’s not about decoration — it’s about depth.
